name: Madeira Chicken with Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 2 boned and skinned chicken breast halves
4 teaspoons Butter Buds (or 4 teaspoons butter-flavored canola oil)
1/4 cup Madeira or dry sherry (or less)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the mushroom sauce by combining the milk, cornstarch, sage, salt, and pepper in a separate bowl.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
3. Cook chicken in batches for about 5 to 6 minutes on each side until no longer pink. Remove from the skillet and keep warm.
4. In the same skillet, add water and butter-granules (or butter-flavored canola oil). Heat until boiling.
5. Add sliced mushrooms and cook for about 2 minutes or until tender and heated through.
6. Stir in the milk mixture and continue cooking for an additional 2 minutes to reduce slightly.
7. Add Madeira or dry sherry (at least 1 tablespoon) and heat until the alcohol evaporates, achieving the desired sauce consistency.
8. Transfer chicken to a warmed serving platter, spooning the sauce over top, and sprinkle with chopped chives.
9. Serve with steamed baby carrots and an aromatic rice (Tex-Mati).
10. For four servings, use 4 3-ounce pieces of chicken breast, pounded slightly for even thickness. Do not increase the sauce.
11. Pair with a clear soup like chicken and fresh spinach or a Caesar salad, along with carrots and snaps, plenty of basmati rice with slivered almonds and chopped fresh cilantro, and a tart apple pie.
